mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income that drives consumer spending and corporate revenue surprises, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS growth versus 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises equity discount rates faster than earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% episodes produce positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 50% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising consumer prices and a restrictive federal funds rate produces a stagflationary impulse that compresses real corporate earnings; jointly elevated CPI and Fed funds predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) over main effects in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production accelerations predict negative S&P 500 returns during monetary tightening cycles because supply constraints amplify input cost pressures when credit is restrictive, leading to margin compression over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth coincides with positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 50% of tightening episodes where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.75%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales momentum (RSAFS) reflects household spending strength that translates directly into corporate revenue surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.04 against 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% that is declining over the prior three months signals an overheating labor market that squeezes corporate margins through accelerating wage costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% and declining over prior 3 months
failure_condition: UNRATE below 4.0% and falling produces positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead the business cycle through residential construction multiplier effects on employment and materials demand; accelerating starts predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth quartiles show no monotonic relationship with 60-day SPX returns (Jonckheere-Terpstra p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) captures aggregate demand expansion that drives broad-based corporate earnings growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no significant positive correlation (Pearson r < 0.05) with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over a 10-year sample.
